_comment: actions_catalog.json — single source of truth for all BattleLuck flow actions.

_usage.registered: Flat list of every action name the executor recognises. Add new names here when you add a case to FlowActionExecutor.
_usage.examples: Parameterised example strings grouped by category — used by dev tools, AI, and autocomplete.
_usage.runtime_inject: Per-event action lists injected while the event is RUNNING. Edit this section live; the executor hot-reads it each check. Key = modeId (e.g. 'bloodbath'). Value = array of action strings to execute on next inject tick.

rules: When generating new JSON/actions, prefer canonical action names unless the user explicitly asks for a legacy alias.
Keep legacy aliases available for old configs and imported events.
For destructive/controlled actions, use preview/approval flows unless an admin explicitly enables auto execution.
Prefer announce/notification for LLM autonomous output; combat, spawn, build, and cleanup actions should be reviewed first.
For castle/build work, search first with build.search or palette.add, then use build.spawn:prefab=palette for visual tracked objects.
Live schematics, blueprints, tile/floor/wall construction, platforms, and native geometry destruction are disabled by the strict server-stability profile.
Use logical zone radii, boundary damage/teleport, markers, announcements, and existing world terrain instead of spawning arena geometry.
Do not put teleport actions in event enter flows; SessionController teleports players after the logical preparation and warmup gates complete.
No entrant may initialize live geometry. The first entrant initializes session state only; later players join that same logical session.

Event entry snapshots and removes the old kit, equipment, passives, and ability overrides; event exit removes the event kit before restoring the original snapshot. Never place kit.apply or ability mutations after snapshot.restore.
livesPerPlayer is an in-arena respawn allowance: 3 means three immediate arena respawns, then elimination and personal rollback on the next death.
Tech/progression mutations and entity.destroy are disabled inside events; do not propose or execute them.
Before proposing or executing a live action, validate catalog registration, handler availability, mutation policy, count caps, and prompt policy. Omit or skip any rejected action.
Use merchant.run and merchant.grant for merchant item tokens that map a bought/acquired item prefab or item GUID to a numbered UUID listing in merchant_servant_actions.json.

notify: announce:title=Setup|message={mode} arena is preparing for {players} player(s).|color=#5CC8FF|level=info

announce.description: Send a colored in-game event announcement. Supports color/rgb/hex, level, title, scope, and placeholders like {mode}, {zone}, {players}, {session}.

merchant.run.description: Execute a configured numbered merchant UUID listing for the current player. Listings live in merchant_servant_actions.json and can rent bosses, teleport players, or run admin-only action bundles.

merchant.grant.description: Grant the configured item token for a merchant UUID listing. When token scanning is enabled, the token activates after it appears in the player's inventory.

ability.set_slot.notes: Only Q/E/R slots are allowed. Travel/T is controlled by kit config.

system.find.description: Hot-reloaded event/admin action that asks AI to explain matching ECS/ProjectM/Unity/EOS systems from the KindredExtract reference inventory. Safe for runtime_inject and flow JSON.

system.search.description: Local-only KindredExtract reference search. Does not call AI, useful when providers are offline.

system.register.description: Admin-approved live registration of an exact ProjectM or Unity ECS system from the local KindredExtract inventory. Registration creates a verified BattleLuck reference/action only; it never creates, patches, or invokes a native ECS system.

spawn.boss.description: Spawn and register a controlled event boss. Bloodbath bosses should normally use neutralUntilStart=true and protectTeam=true so they do not attack before the match and cannot hurt their assigned team.

npc.follow.notes: selector can be npcId, near, near:radius, pos:x,y,z:radius, all, or a prefab/display-name match. position+radius selects NPCs for non-goto actions; selectPosition/selectRadius is preferred.

npc.goto.notes: For npc.goto, position/destination is the move target. Use selectPosition/selectRadius or selector=pos:x,y,z:radius to choose NPCs by area.

palette.add.notes: Adds the best live build/tile prefab match to the requesting player's build palette. Use before build.spawn:prefab=palette.

schematic.destroy.radius.notes: Compatibility alias for tracked-only cleanup. Never sweeps or destroys untracked world buildings, floors, walls, or items.

progression.unlock.all_vbloods.notes: Progression unlock request only. Do not add boss-lock or altar-block patches.

progression.unlock.all_research.notes: Progression unlock request only. Avoid permanent mutation unless rollback support exists.

sequence.custom.preview.description: Preview a reusable BattleLuck custom sequence without executing it.

sequence.custom.play.description: Run a named custom sequence from custom_sequences.json. In unified event runtime, timed steps are queued on the session clock.

sequence.step.run.description: Execute a specific step from a registered sequence by sequenceId and stepId.

sequence.step.skip.description: Skip a specific step in a registered sequence without executing it.

sequence.step.retry.description: Retry a specific step in a registered sequence, logging the retry attempt.

objective.deliver.description: Require a player to carry an optional item to a target position, remove the item, award player/team score, and emit objective captured.

death.prevent.notes: Consumes one charge per prevented death. Parameters: initialCharges, activeWindowSeconds, triggerCooldownSeconds, onTriggeredSequenceId.

clan.task.add.description: Creates a persistent world or event clan task. Player assignment uses stable Steam IDs.

clan.task.update.description: Adds trusted admin progress to an active clan task.

clan.task.progress.description: Adds trusted admin progress to an active clan task. Generic inventory transfers never call this automatically.

clan.task.reward.description: Internal completion action that dispatches the configured reward through the catalog-backed score action.

sequences.name: Bloodbath Intro
sequences.description: Standard intro sequence for Bloodbath mode.
